commit.message:
Implement sequential PR review pipeline: Architect → TE → PM → merge

Restructure PR approval flow to match real engineering workflows:

Phase 1 - Architect Review:
- Architect adds 'architect-approved' label on approval (no longer merges)
- Skips PRs already architect-approved
- Sends StatusUpdate to notify TE

Phase 2 - Test Engineer:
- TE scans for 'architect-approved' label instead of 'approved'
- Adds tests, screenshots, videos, then 'tests-added' label

Phase 3 - PM Final Review:
- PM only reviews PRs with 'tests-added' label (Phase 3 gate)
- PM proactively scans for Phase 3-ready PRs each cycle
- Enhanced AI prompt includes visual validation of TE screenshots/videos
- PM posts '[ProgramManager] APPROVED' and adds 'pm-approved' label
- PM no longer calls ApproveAndMaybeMergeAsync (decoupled from merge)

Merge Gate:
- PE's MergeTestedPRsAsync now checks for 'pm-approved' + 'tests-added'
- Only PE merges, and only after PM final approval

Config:
- Added MaxArchitectReworkCycles (default: 3) to LimitsConfig
- Added MaxPmReworkCycles (default: 3) to LimitsConfig
- New labels: architect-approved, pm-approved in PullRequestWorkflow.Labels

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): bump Go to 1.25 and setup-go to v6.4.0

The provider's go.mod requires Go 1.25.0, but CI installed 1.23.
With setup-go v6's stricter toolchain enforcement, the build correctly
rejects the version mismatch — causing validate, unit, and integration
jobs to fail.

to v6.4.0 in all three affected jobs.

commit.message:
docs: expand PUB5 with two-category upgrade design

Distinguish two fundamental upgrade categories:

Category 1 (infrastructure): dactor/adapter wire protocol changes.
Nodes cannot form a single cluster. Requires cluster split (blue/green).
Connection handshake rejects incompatible protocol versions immediately.

Category 2 (application): dactor unchanged, app code changes.
Nodes can cluster normally, system actors work. Remote actor calls
may fail if message schemas changed. MessageVersionHandler handles
backward-compatible migrations. Rolling restart within one cluster.

Detection: wire protocol version + application version in handshake
determines which category automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: production hardening — reliability, performance, observability, infra

Backend:
- Add structured JSON logging with request ID tracing (logger.ts)
- Add graceful shutdown (SIGTERM/SIGINT) with connection draining
- Add process.on(unhandledRejection/uncaughtException) crash guards
- Health check now verifies DB + Redis connectivity, returns 503 if down
- Background jobs wrapped with error handling (.catch on all imports)
- Scheduled expired refresh token cleanup (every 6 hours)
- Analytics: replace all findMany→in-memory with DB aggregation (prevents OOM)
  - Daily revenue/orders via GROUP BY DATE
  - PageView analytics via COUNT(DISTINCT sessionId), GROUP BY country/path
  - Order funnel via groupBy paymentStatus
  - Hourly orders via EXTRACT(HOUR)
  - Previous period via aggregate() instead of findMany()
- Prisma client: add query logging for dev, error/warn for production
- Abandoned cart queries: add orderBy to prevent reprocessing same records

Database:
- Add missing indexes: Order(paymentStatus, createdAt, composite),
  OrderItem(productId, orderId), Payment(status, orderId),
  Cart(createdAt, updatedAt), User(createdAt, role)

Frontend:
- ServiceWorker: register SW properly instead of unregistering all (fixes PWA)
- API client: 30s AbortController timeout on all requests
- API client: exponential backoff retry (3 attempts) for 5xx/network errors
- API client: respect caller AbortSignal for request cancellation

Infrastructure:
- Docker: Redis AOF persistence + 256MB maxmemory + LRU eviction + volume
- Docker: PostgreSQL tuning (shared_buffers, work_mem, max_connections, etc.)
- Docker: CPU/memory resource limits on all services
- Docker: DB connection pool params (connection_limit=20, pool_timeout=30s)
- Docker: internal network isolation (only nginx exposed)
- Nginx: gzip compression for text/css/js/json/svg/woff2
- Nginx: static asset caching (30d, immutable)
- Nginx: proxy buffering, rate limiting zones, SSL session cache/stapling
- Nginx: separate API route block with rate limiting
- Nginx: additional security headers (Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy)
- Dockerfile: strict TypeScript build (no error suppression)
- Dockerfile: production-only deps (npm ci --omit=dev) — smaller image
- Dockerfile: prisma migrate deploy instead of db push --accept-data-loss
- Dockerfile: run as non-root user (appuser:nodejs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
